title: Clamp a Number
description: Write a Python function that clamps a number between a minimum and maximum value. If the number is below min_val, return min_val. If it is above max_val, return max_val. Otherwise return the number unchanged.
reference_solution:
def clamp(n, min_val, max_val):
    return max(min_val, min(max_val, n))
clarification: Return the number unchanged if it falls within [min_val, max_val]. If it is below min_val, return min_val; if above max_val, return max_val. Example: clamp(5, 1, 10) → 5, clamp(0, 1, 10) → 1, clamp(15, 1, 10) → 10.
